title: lomatium lucidum var. howellii
intro: Lomatium lucidum var. howellii, also known as Howells' Biscuitroot, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and edible roots. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as colds and flu. The health benefits of Lomatium lucidum var. howellii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and capsules to harness its medicinal properties, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
